questions.question: What is the length of perpendicular from point (8, -3) on the line 4y = 3x + 14?
questions.explanation: The formula for finding the foot of perpendicular from point (x1, y1) on the line (ax + by + c = 0) is- (x - x1)/a = (y - y1)/b = -(ax1 + by1 + c)/(a2 + b2) (ax + by + c = 0) = (3x - 4y + 14) and (x1, y1) = (8, -3) After comparing- a = 3, b = -4, c = 14, x1 = 8 and y1 = -3 From the equation- (x - 8)/3 = (y + 3)/(-4) = -[3 * 8 + (-4) * (-3) + 14]/[32 + (-4)2 (x - 8)/3 = (y + 3)/(-4) = -2 (x - 8)/3 = -2 (x - 8) = -6 x = 2 (y + 3)/(-4) = -2 (y + 3) = 8 y = 5 Required co-ordinates = (x, y) = (2, 5) Distance between two points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2)- √[(x2 - x1)2+ (y2 - y1)2] Required distance = √[(8 - 2)2+ (-3 - 5)2] = = √[62+ 82] = 10 unit Alternative Solution: Length of perpendicular from point (x1, y1) on the line ax+by+c = 0 is │ax1+by1+c│/√[a2+ b2] = │3*8-4*-3+14│/√[32+ 42] = 10

questions.question: If total questions in exam was 120, then what is the number of questions asked in the exam was from Civics section?
questions.explanation: Total questions in exam = 120 Total number of questions asked from Civics section = 120 * (72/360) = 24

questions.question: Total number of questions asked from Geography section exceeds by how much from questions asked in History section if questions from Polity section is 30.
questions.explanation: Total questions from Polity section = 900 = 30 Total number of questions from Geography section = 30 * (54/90) = 18 Total number of questions from History section = 30 * (36/90) = 12 Required difference = 18 - 12 = 6

questions.question: Total questions asked from Polity and Civics section together is what per cent of total number of questions asked in the exam?
questions.explanation: Total questions asked from Polity and Civics section together = 900 + 720 = 1620 Total number of questions asked in the exam = 3600 Required per cent = (162/360) * 100 = 45%

questions.question: Total questions asked from Current Affairs section is 18, then what is the difference between total questions asked from History and Polity?
questions.explanation: Total questions from Current Affairs section = 18 Total questions from History = 18 * (36/54) = 12 Total questions from Polity = 18 * (90/54) = 30 Required difference = 30 - 12 = 18

questions.question: If y/x + x/y = 2, what is the value of (5x - 2y)/3y? [x, y ≠ 0]

The number of pigeons in a farm is 25% greater than the number of cows and 20% lesser than the number of goats. If the farm has only pigeons, cows and goats and the number of legs in the farm is 1020, what is the sum of the number of pigeons and goats?
questions.explanation: Let the number of pigeons in the farm be 'x' Number of cows in the farm = (100/125)*x = 4x/5 Number of goats in the farm = (100/80)*x = 5x/4 So, 2x + 4*(4x/5) + 4(5x/4) = 1020 => 2x + 16x/5 + 5x = 1020 => 35x + 16x = 1020*5 => 51x = 1020*5 => x = 100 Sum of the number of pigeons and goats = x + 5x/4 = 100 + (5/4)*100 = 225

questions.question: Amit's age 6 years from now will be 26 years more than 75% of the age of Amit 12 years ago. What is his present age?
questions.explanation: Let the present age of Amit be 'x' years So, (x + 6) - (75/100)*(x - 12) = 26 x - (3/4)*(x - 12) = 20 4x - 3x + 36 = 80 x = 44

questions.question: From 40 litres of pure alcohol, 4 litres is taken out and replaced with water. This process is done one more time and then 1.8 litres of alcohol is added. What is the final ratio of alcohol and water in the solution?
questions.explanation: After replacing with water 2 times and before adding 1.8 litres of alcohol Quantity of alcohol in the solution = 40(1 - 4/40)2 = 32.4 litres Quantity of water in the solution = 40 - 32.4 = 7.6 litres Final quantity of alcohol in the solution = 32.4 + 1.8 = 34.2 litres Required ratio = 34.2:7.6 = 9:2

questions.question: If 4B/A = 3C/A = 12, then what is the value of (1/A): (1/B): (1/C)?
What is the simplified form of 24 ÷ 3 of (21 ÷ 14 of 18) + 36 - 20% of 75?
If 5z1y9 is divisible by 9, then find the minimum value of (y + z).
questions.explanation: 5z1y9 is divisible by 9 means sum of all digits of given number is divisible by 9., i.e., = (5 + z + 1 + y + 9) is divisible by 9 = (15 + z + y) is divisible by 9 Multiple of 9 which is greater than 15 is 18. So, minimum value of (y + z) = 18 - 15 = 3

questions.question: One root of equation x2 - px + q = 0 is (8 - 5√2), then what is the value of 'p + q'?
questions.explanation: If one root of equation is (8 - 5√2), then other roots must be (8 + 5√2). Equation whose roots are (8 - 5√2) and (8 + 5√2) = x2 - sum of roots * x + Product of roots = 0 Sum of the roots = (8 - 5√2) + (8 + 5√2) = 16 Product of the roots = (8 - 5√2) * (8 + 5√2) = 64 - 50 = 14 Required equation = x2 - 16x + 14 After comparing, p = 16 and q = 14 Required answer = 'p + q' = 16 + 14 = 30

questions.question: The angle of elevation of the tip of the shadow of a tree with respect to the top of the tree of height 6m is 30°. After 30 minutes, the angle of elevation of the same becomes 60°. What is the change in the length of the shadow in this duration?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/rrbimg/54164/image004.jpg) In triangle ABC, tan30° = 6/BC => BC = 6√3 in triangle ABD, tan60° = 6/BD => BD = 6/√3 = 2√3 Change in length of shadow = CD = BC-BD = 4√3m

questions.question: A sum of Rs. 1230 is borrowed at 5% C.l. interest for 2 years on the condition that it is refunded in 2 equal installments one at the end of the first year & the other at the end of the second. What is the amount of each installment.
questions.explanation: Let the installments be Rs. x each Amount to be refunded = 1230(1 + 5/100)2 = 1356.075 x(1 + 5/100) + x = 1356.075 x = Rs. 661.50

questions.question: The average of three positive integers A, B and C is equal to an integer D. The average of A and D is another integer E. If the average of A and C is 5 more than E, what is the difference between D and the average of A and B?
If a = (√6+ 1)/(√6-1) and b = (√6- 1)/(√6+1) Then what is the value of (a2 + b2 + ab )/(a2 + b2- ab ) ?

If a rectangular sheet of paper having sides 15 cm and 44 cm is shaped in the form of a cylinder such that the side of length 15 cm becomes height of cylinder, then what will be the volume of cylinder?
questions.explanation: After converting in to a cylinder, 44 cm is the circumference of the cylinder. Radius= (44/(2π)) = 7 cm. Volume of cylinder= π x (7 x 7) x 15 = 22 x 7 x 15 =330 x 7 = 2310 cm3

questions.question: What is the value of expression Tan2θ + Cot2θ - Sec2θCosec2θ?
questions.explanation: Tan2θ + Cot2θ - Sec2θCosec2θ => Sin2θ/Cos2θ + Cos2θ/Sin2θ - 1/Cos2θ * 1/Sin2θ => [Sin4θ + Cos4θ - 1]/Sin2θ Cos2θ => [Sin4θ + Cos4θ + 2Sin2θCos2θ - 1 - 2Sin2θCos2θ]/Sin2θ Cos2θ => [(Sin2θ + Cos2θ)2 - 1 - 2Sin2θCos2θ]/Sin2θCos2θ => (-2Sin2θCos2θ)/Sin2θCos2θ => -2

questions.question: In the following figure AP and AQ are two tangents and point O is the centre of the circle. Find the angle between tangent AP and AQ. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p2920/image002.png)
questions.explanation: ∠OPQ = 350 (Given) ∠OPQ = ∠OQP = 350 (Angles opposite to equal sides are also equal) ∠POQ = 1800 - 350 - 350 = 1100 ∠PAQ = 1800 - ∠POQ (In quadrilateral PAQO, ∠OQA = ∠OPA = 900) ∠PAQ = 1800 - 1100 = 700

questions.question: A shopkeeper marks his goods in such a way that the profit on sale of 30 refined oil packets is equal to the selling price of 15 refined oil packets. What is his profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Let cost price of 1 refined oil packet be C Let selling price of 1 refined oil packet be S According to question: 30*(S - C) = 15*S 2S - 2C = S S = 2C Profit percent = (S-C)/C * 100 = (2C - C)/C * 100 = 100 %

questions.question: If 20 men worked for 5 hours a day, they can build a 190 feet long wall in 10 days. If after working for 6 days, 4 men leave the job, then how many days will be required to complete rest of the job if the number of working hours per day is the same?
questions.explanation: 20 men working 5 hours of a day can build a wall in 10 days. These 20 men worked for 6 days. This implies that they have built 6/10 i.e., 60% of the wall in 6 days. Since, the length of the wall is 190 feet, part of wall completed in 6 days = 0.6 X 190 = 114 feet Now, Amount of wall still left = 190 - 114 = 76 feet. Also, the number of men left = 20 - 4 = 16 The rate of working is same in both the cases. Let the number of days now required be D. 114/76 = (20 X 5 X 6)/(16 X 5 X D) D = 5 days

questions.question: What is the unit digit of 31286 + 12529?
questions.explanation: The concept of cyclicity is used to identify the last digit of the number. Cyclicity chart: NumberCyclicityUnit digit values Power 1Power 2Power 3Power 4 010 111 242486 343971 4246 515 616 747931 848426 9291 (Note: remainder = power. When remainder is 0, power = cyclicity) In 31286: Unit digit of 312 is 2. 286: From the cyclicity chart, cyclicity of 2 is 4. 86/4 remainder =2 = power 2 So, unit digit of 31286 = 4 In 12529: Unit digit of 125 is 5. 529: From the cyclicity chart, cyclicity of 5 is 1. Unit digit of 12529 = 5 Therefore, unit digit of 31286 + 12529 = unit digit of (4 + 5) = 9

questions.question: Sum of two numbers is equal to sum of square of 15 and cube of 7. One of the numbers is 52 less than square of 18. What is the value of the square of the difference between the numbers?
questions.explanation: Let the numbers be a and b. a+b = 152 + 73 = 225+343 = 568 a = 182 - 52 = 272 => b = 296 Required number = (296-272)2 = 576

questions.question: In a 4 km race on a course of 250 m round, A overlaps B in the middle of his 5th round. By what distance will A win at the same rate of running? Given that they start simultaneously from same point and in the same direction.
questions.explanation: A overlaps B in the middle of his 5th round. So, when A runs 4(1/2) rounds, B runs only 3(1/2) rounds (A's speed/ B's speed) = (4(1/2) / 3 (1/2)) = 9/7 When A runs 4 km, B runs 4 * (7/9) = 28/9 km A wins by (4 - 28/9) = 8/9 km
